audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Abraham/Sarah sister-wife repetition, Abimelech dream-warning, innocence/integrity language, prophet/intercession, womb-closing, and restoration. Final review may refine legal idioms.

verses.restored: Abraham journeyed from there toward the land of the Negev and dwelled between Kadesh and Shur; he sojourned in Gerar.

familiar.text: And Abraham journeyed from thence toward the south country, and dwelled between Kadesh and Shur...

verses.restored: Abraham said of Sarah his woman, She is my sister. Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.

familiar.text: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister: and Abimelech king of Gerar sent, and took Sarah.

notes.title: Repeated sister-wife pattern
notes.body: The episode echoes Genesis 12. Sarah is again endangered by Abraham’s fear and partial truth.

verses.restored: But the Divine came to Abimelech in a dream by night and said to him, Look, you are a dead man because of the woman you have taken, for she is married to a husband.

familiar.text: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, Behold, thou art but a dead man...

notes.title: Dream warning
notes.body: The Divine protects Sarah through direct warning to Abimelech.

verses.restored: Now Abimelech had not come near her. He said, Lord, will you kill even a righteous nation?

familiar.text: But Abimelech had not come near her: and he said, Lord, wilt thou slay also a righteous nation?

verses.restored: Did he not say to me, She is my sister? And she, even she, said, He is my brother. In the integrity of my heart and the cleanness of my hands I have done this.

familiar.text: Said he not unto me, She is my sister? and she, even she herself said...

notes.title: Integrity and clean hands
notes.body: Abimelech appeals to inner integrity and outward innocence.

verses.restored: The Divine said to him in the dream, Yes, I know that in the integrity of your heart you did this. I also held you back from sinning against me; therefore I did not let you touch her.

familiar.text: And God said unto him in a dream, Yea, I know that thou didst this in the integrity of thy heart...

notes.title: Held back from sinning
notes.body: The Divine prevents Abimelech from crossing the line, preserving Sarah and Abimelech alike.

verses.restored: Now return the man’s woman, for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you and you shall live. But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours.

familiar.text: Now therefore restore the man his wife; for he is a prophet, and he shall pray for thee...

notes.title: Prophet
notes.body: This is the first explicit use of prophet in the Hebrew Bible, applied to Abraham in an intercessory role.

verses.restored: Abimelech rose early in the morning, called all his servants, and spoke all these words in their ears. The men were very afraid.

familiar.text: Therefore Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants...

verses.restored: Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought upon me and my kingdom a great offense? You have done things to me that should not be done.

familiar.text: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said unto him, What hast thou done unto us?...

notes.title: What have you done?
notes.body: The accountability formula again confronts Abraham’s deception.

verses.restored: Abimelech said to Abraham, What did you see, that you did this thing?

familiar.text: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What sawest thou, that thou hast done this thing?

verses.restored: Abraham said, Because I said, Surely there is no fear of the Divine in this place, and they will kill me because of my woman.

familiar.text: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely the fear of God is not in this place...

notes.title: Fear of the Divine
notes.body: Abraham’s fear about the place reveals his assumption and his self-protective deception.

verses.restored: And also, she truly is my sister, daughter of my father but not daughter of my mother, and she became my woman.

familiar.text: And yet indeed she is my sister; she is the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother...

notes.title: Partial truth
notes.body: Abraham explains the statement as kinship truth, but the narrative still exposes its deceptive function.

verses.restored: When the Divine caused me to wander from my father’s house, I said to her, This is your covenant-kindness that you shall do for me: at every place where we come, say of me, He is my brother.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when God caused me to wander from my father's house...

notes.title: Caused me to wander
notes.body: Abraham describes his call as wandering from father’s house. The sister claim was an ongoing survival strategy.

verses.restored: Abimelech took sheep, cattle, male servants, and female servants and gave them to Abraham, and he returned Sarah his woman to him.

familiar.text: And Abimelech took sheep, and oxen, and menservants, and womenservants...

verses.restored: Abimelech said, Look, my land is before you. Dwell wherever it is good in your eyes.

familiar.text: And Abimelech said, Behold, my land is before thee: dwell where it pleaseth thee.

verses.restored: To Sarah he said, Look, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver. Look, it is a covering of eyes for you before all who are with you, and before everyone you are vindicated.

familiar.text: And unto Sarah he said, Behold, I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver...

notes.title: Covering of eyes
notes.body: A difficult idiom likely marking public vindication or compensation. Abimelech pointedly calls Abraham your brother.

verses.restored: Abraham prayed to the Divine, and the Divine healed Abimelech, his woman, and his female servants, and they bore children.

familiar.text: So Abraham prayed unto God: and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maidservants...

notes.title: Intercession and healing
notes.body: Abraham’s prophetic role is fulfilled through prayer that restores fertility.

verses.restored: For the Eternal had completely closed every womb of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham’s woman.

familiar.text: For the LORD had fast closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech, because of Sarah...
